steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
The first part — "The sand at Vermelha Beach in Brazil is a strikingly different shade" — is an independent clause.

What follows it ("though most sand gets its shade from...quartz and feldspar, deposits of iron oxide...lend the sand...a colorful red tint") is also an independent clause that explains how the shade is different.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon can join an independent clause to a follow-up that explains or elaborates on it — exactly the relationship here.

shade, though most creates a comma splice, B inserts an ungrammatical comma after "though," and D leaves a sentence fragment.

Only the colon in C correctly introduces the elaboration.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
